notes_for_human_reviewer: SOLO-REWRITER FAILURE MODE — the symmetric opposite of diagram_polisher. The learner has internalised the 100%-rule omission categories via the rewrite (Training, Change Mgmt, Hypercare, Transition, Closure all appear as new branches) and partly fixed deliverable-orientation (some leaves are now nouns) but commits the central process error: rewriting the WBS solo and declaring it ready to baseline ('I think this is now ready to baseline directly'), explicitly waving off the workshop/walkthrough ('I do not think the leads will have meaningful additions to make'). This is the chapter's anti-pattern in full — treating the WBS as a deliverable to be produced rather than a shared model to be built. Caps fire hard on collaborative_process_recognition (1) — proposes 'courtesy review' as the alternative to substantive team validation, framed dismissively. cross_artifact_traceability also caps at 1 because the rewrite focuses on the artefact itself with no propagation reasoning. Decomposition tolerance widened to 2 because the rewrite is internally reasonable but uses the learner's own judgement rather than the doers' input — the chapter's exact failure mode. Tests whether the grader catches competent-looking work that exhibits the central process error. Note: hundred_percent_rule_compliance widened to score=2 tol=2 because Claude correctly applies the 'audit, do not rewrite' framing strictly — the rewrite is read as inventing rather than auditing, capping the dim hard.
